title: Research: do jmp condition-suffix mnemonics work? (Appendix B p.276 vs assembler error)
notes: Empirical research, no production change. Question: Appendix B p.276 says jmp takes the branch condition suffixes (jmpz/jmpn/...), but they error on assembly. Tested both tools: lccjs rejects jmpz/jmpn/jmpe with Invalid operation; oracle (cuh63 6.3) rejects jmpz IDENTICALLY — the leftover 7-byte .e is the deviation-10 blank artifact, not a real assembly (initial script misread the .e existence as acceptance; corrected). Static: assembler.js has no jmpXX cases, suffixes belong only to br; jmp encoding has no cc field. Conclusion: textbook DOC ERROR, not an lccjs gap — lccjs is parity-correct, no assembler fix. Recorded docs/research/jmp-condition-suffix-mnemonics.md, added a note to docs/lcc-isa.md, resolved the @todo #151 marker in happy-path.a. Note: happy-path.a exits 1 on a pre-existing brb error (line 43) on pristine main too — not caused here, out of scope. Branch needed a rebase onto apple #182/#183 before push (clean). Worktree (banana).

title: Test: linker aborts WITHOUT writing output on unresolved external (regression guard)

title: Test: linker createExecutable() byte format (signature/header/terminator + LE code words)
notes: Fifth/final child of the #171 spike — completes the epic. 2 tests in linker.unit.spec.js driving the previously-mocked createExecutable() through the virtualFs helper and asserting exact output bytes: o-signature, S+start(LE), G+addr+label+NUL, VTable-emitted-as-'A' entries BEFORE ATable 'A' entries (the spike's format refinement), C terminator, little-endian uint16 code; plus no 'S' without gotStart. title: Research: CSV velocity log → merge=union + empty closed_commit (kill rebase-conflict churn)
notes: Spike for #186. Empirically verified in a throwaway repo that the built-in `union` merge driver FIRES UNDER git rebase (not just merge): two divergent branches each appending a CSV row auto-unioned with 0 conflict markers; same test confirmed the rebase still rewrites the commit SHA (79e238c->ca419d5), so merge=union fixes the manual-conflict pain (A) but NOT the closed_commit-orphan pain (B) — orthogonal. Recommendation (docs/research/velocity-log-storage.md): keep the CSV, add 'docs/puzzle-velocity.csv merge=union' to .gitattributes, switch to a single-commit close, log closed_commit EMPTY and derive via git log --grep 'Closes #N'. SQLite rejected (binary: unmergeable, undiffable, conflicts worse). Applied: .gitattributes line + docs/puzzle-velocity.md protocol/column rewrite + puzzle-velocity skill 0.6.0 in claude-config. THIS ROW dogfoods the new close: single commit, closed_commit intentionally empty. Follow-up: bulk closed_commit backfill reconciler. actual incl. collaborative scope review. Worked as cherry alongside live agents. [role reclassified RESEARCH->COMBO 2026-05-29 #192: original tag was RESEARCH; see role-table COMBO/SPIKE defs.]

title: Research: claim auto-fruit reuses a fruit whose agent session is still alive
notes: Spike confirming banana's root cause and recommending a fix. takenFruits() (scripts/claim.js) is worktree-scoped, not session-scoped: a fruit frees the instant its last worktree is removed, so an agent sitting between puzzles (alive, no worktree) is invisible and auto can hand its name to another agent. Key non-obvious finding: reconciling the code to its own header comment (scan all <fruit>/* branches instead of worktree list) would NOT fix it, because close deletes the branch too — only a session-lifetime artifact closes the gap. The design doc already listed 'fruit reuse after teardown' as an accepted edge case asserting 'never concurrently'; #193 is the counterexample that falsifies it (worktree-liveness != session-liveness). Options weighed: (A) session-sentinel branch — principled, keeps git as source of truth, recommended as deferred DEV puzzle if it recurs; (B) lockfile/registry — rejected, the design's explicit non-goal; (C) time-window — rejected, fragile; (D) mandatory --as for concurrent fan-out — recommended NOW at zero cost (already how the project operates). Deliverable: docs/research/claim-fruit-session-scope.md + an annotation on the design doc's edge-case bullet. No code shipped (severity:low, logging mitigation already exists). Proposed follow-ups: deferred DEV (Option A) + cheap DOCS (fix the claim.js header comment / design-doc source-of-truth claim). No marker (markdown-only research issue). Verified auto returns 'cherry' while apple+banana hold worktrees.

Filing the 5 issues required explicit user authorization (auto-classifier blocked the first attempt as a scope escalation; user confirmed via AskUserQuestion). #166 closed as tracker; TODOS checkbox updated to link children; no code/tests written here by design. actual_min is hands-on time; wall-clock (started->finished, ~65m) spans the classifier-block + ask-user decision pause and is NOT the work duration (cf. #141). closed_commit empty per #186. Worktree cherry-issue-166; terminal name CHERRY.
